title: celtica gigantea subsp. donyanae
intro: Celtica gigantea subsp. donyanae, also known as Donyana's celtic or giant celtis, is a rare subspecies of Celtis gigantea, a type of deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Celtica gigantea subsp. donyanae are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to the prevention of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. In traditional herbal medicine, Celtica gigantea subsp. donyanae is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in topical preparations, to take advantage of its therapeutic properties.
